typecheck.detail: no issues found in 44 source files

nonBlockingIssues.description: Unix file permissions not preserved through atomic rename. Temp file inherits default permissions instead of original file's. Unlikely to affect typical source files but could surprise users with non-default permissions (e.g., 0o755 executable scripts).
Redundant 'use std::convert::TryFrom' import (already in Rust 2021 prelude). Harmless but unnecessary.
docs/examples/rewrite_plan.json not updated with new planned_mtime_ns field. Schema compat test handles this via Option<u64> + serde(default), so no breakage, but the reference example is incomplete.

Inherent TOCTOU gap in parallel write pattern: batch staleness preflight passes, then parallel writes begin. If a file is modified in the narrow window between preflight and per-file check, other files may already have been written. Per-file defense-in-depth catches the stale file but cannot roll back already-written peers. Acceptable for lock-free design.

Minor double-read inefficiency: load_rewrite_source() reads 8KB prefix for binary detection, then reads full file again. Could be optimized to a single read, but current approach avoids full allocation for binary files.

docs/routing_policy.md step ordering and 'caveat' about warm-index preempting GPU are now stale after commit f8ef2f2 reordered GPU before warm-index. Doc and test assertion in test_routing_policy_docs.py need updating.
test_routing_stale_index_with_explicit_index_rebuilds uses 50ms sleep for mtime advancement. Safe on NTFS but potentially flaky on FAT32 or network filesystems.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Remove or mark as resolved the 'Known Pre-Existing Issues' bullet about warm index routing preceding GPU routing. This was fixed by commit f8ef2f2 in the routing-regression-tests feature.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md 'Known Pre-Existing Issues' says 'Warm index routing precedes GPU routing...The routing-regression-tests feature should fix this.' Commit f8ef2f2 moved explicit --gpu-device-ids check before warm-index auto-routing in main.rs handle_ripgrep_search.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: when a documentation feature (like routing-policy-docs) lands concurrently with code features that modify the documented behavior, the doc should be re-validated against HEAD after the code feature lands. This prevents docs from going stale within the same milestone.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: routing-policy-docs (commit d55fef3) documented warm-index before GPU order. routing-regression-tests (commit f8ef2f2) reordered these. The doc was accurate at its commit time but immediately stale when the next commit landed.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: the rewrite pipeline uses a two-layer staleness defense pattern (batch preflight in apply_rewrites/plan_and_apply + per-file check in apply_edits_to_file). Workers modifying the rewrite pipeline should preserve both layers.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Features stale-file-detection and encoding-safety both modify backend_ast.rs rewrite paths. The defense-in-depth pattern was added by stale-file-detection and must be preserved by future modifications.

rejectedObservations.observation: The rust-worker skill's example handoff mentions stale-file detection alongside atomic writes but these are separate features.
rejectedObservations.reason: The skill example is illustrative, not normative. No change needed — the worker correctly followed the procedure regardless.

rejectedObservations.observation: The search path (search_file_static) does not have encoding safety guards (no BOM handling, binary detection, large-file guard).
rejectedObservations.reason: This is correct per feature scope. Encoding safety targets only the rewrite path. The search path delegates to rg/ast-grep which have their own handling. No action needed for this milestone.

rejectedObservations.observation: architecture.md in .factory/library/ already documents stale-file detection under Rewrite Safety section.
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented — no duplicate update needed.
